PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current main still has the two casted call sites, and the registry builder already returns the expected map type. I did not rerun the lint lane because this review is read-only, but the PR body includes after-fix Crabbox changed-gate proof.

target.title: fix(agents): remove redundant image provider registry casts
target.body:
## Summary

- remove redundant `providerRegistry` casts in `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`
- restore the current-main core lint lane that was blocking changed-gate proof for #86930 and #86931

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`
- `codex review --base origin/main`
- Crabbox: `run_5d6352699118`, lease `cbx_20ba6ac8c882`, `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -label openclaw-image-tool-oxlint-check-current -shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Rebased after the latest main move and re-ran local `git diff --check` plus targeted core oxlint before pushing head `58a4169dec947a4d66517cfe05d51d0209cc1168`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: current-main `pnpm check:changed` failed in the core lint lane because `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts` kept two unnecessary `providerRegistry` type assertions.

Real environment tested: Crabbox AWS Linux lease `cbx_20ba6ac8c882` from run `run_5d6352699118`, plus local rebase sanity on current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -label openclaw-image-tool-oxlint-check-current -shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; after rebase, `git diff --check` and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`.

Evidence after fix: `pnpm check:changed` selected `core, coreTests`, completed core/core-test typecheck, core lint reported `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`, runtime guard checks passed, and the command exited 0.

Observed result after fix: changed-gate no longer fails on the image tool redundant-cast lint errors.

What was not tested: no live image-provider request; this is a type-only cleanup with no runtime value change.
